name: Aldea del Rey from Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular by the Castillo de Caracuel
name_original: Aldea del Rey desde Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular por el Castillo de Caracuel
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Argamasilla De Calatrava traverse a diverse landscape shaped by volcanic activity and agricultural expanses. The region features undulating roads, vast agricultural fields, vineyards, and olive groves, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Cyclists can explore the Campo de Calatrava volcanic massif, encountering volcanic formations and shallow lagoons. The area also integrates historical sites and charming villages into its network of roads. Best road cycling routes in Argamasilla De Calatrava The most popular road cycling route is Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona, a 72.2 miles (116.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Sierra Madrona. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aldea del Rey from Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular by the Castillo de Caracuel, a moderate 49.9 miles (80.4 km) path. This route offers a mix of agricultural scenery and historical interest, passing by the Castillo de Caracuel. Local road cyclists also love the Mestanza Village – Pantano del Montoro loop from Puertollano, a 53.9 miles (86.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Pantano del Montoro, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Argamasilla De Calatrava is defined by volcanic landscapes, extensive agricultural fields, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through villages to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Argamasilla De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Argamasilla De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Argamasilla de Calatrava?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape shaped by volcanic activity and extensive agricultural areas. You'll encounter undulating roads, vast fields of vineyards and olive groves, and unique volcanic formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Calatrava volcanic massif&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature, providing a distinctive backdrop to your rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in the area?
A: Yes, Argamasilla de Calatrava offers routes with significant elevation gains, especially in areas like the Sierra Madrona. For example, the "&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084847686/puerto-madrona-from-puertollano-circular-via-mestanza-and-the-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona&lt;/a&gt;" route is classified as difficult, featuring over 2,300 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous ride?
A: While many routes have varied terrain, there are options that offer more moderate profiles. The region's agricultural fields and flatter sections provide opportunities for less strenuous rides. Komoot lists 8 easy routes in the area, perfect for a relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I visit while road cycling in the region?
A: The area is rich in history, particularly tied to the Order of Calatrava. You can integrate visits to historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6385676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de la Serna&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084492767/aldea-del-rey-from-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular-by-the-castillo-de-caracuel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Caracuel&lt;/a&gt; into your rides. The town of Argamasilla de Calatrava itself has points of interest such as the Iglesia Parroquial Nuestra Señora de la Visitación.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: The volcanic landscape and agricultural expanses offer many scenic views. You can find picturesque routes through vineyards and olive groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, a natural destination formed by volcanic activity, is a unique natural highlight to explore.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Argamasilla de Calatrava?
A: Yes, circular routes are popular in the region. An example is the "&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084492767/aldea-del-rey-from-argamasilla-de-calatrava-circular-by-the-castillo-de-caracuel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldea del Rey from Argamasilla de Calatrava – circular by the Castillo de Caracuel&lt;/a&gt;" route, which offers a mix of scenery and historical interest. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Argamasilla de Calatrava?
A: The moderate temperatures of spring and autumn make these seasons particularly favorable for road cycling in Argamasilla de Calatrava. The pleasant weather allows you to fully enjoy the natural and historical richness of the area without the extreme heat of summer.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Argamasilla de Calatrava?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from volcanic landscapes to agricultural fields, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical sites and charming villages.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or natural water features?
A: Yes, the region's volcanic past has created shallow lagoons. The "&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestanza Village – Pantano del Montoro loop from Puertollano&lt;/a&gt;" route, for instance, leads through varied terrain including the Pantano del Montoro. You can also find the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7286202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Blanca&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6103586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Caracuel&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What is the total number of road cycling routes available around Argamasilla de Calatrava?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Argamasilla de Calatrava, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to nearby towns or villages?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to connect Argamasilla de Calatrava with nearby towns and villages such as Mestanza, Retamar, Alcolea de Calatrava, and Tirteafuera. This allows for varied journeys and the chance to explore more of the region's local culture and scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of natural beauty and historical elements?
A: Yes, the region is ideal for routes that combine natural landscapes with historical exploration. The "&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6901336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Marina – Turnoff to Virgen de los Santos loop from Argamasilla de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;" route offers a blend of scenic cycling and cultural points of interest like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6612221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de los Santos&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: River that crosses different towns starting from the Sierra de Calatrava between Aldea del Rey and Argamasilla de Calatrava. It is a tributary of the Guadiana River.
From the 14th or early 15th century. Built in three parts. The first one is made up of the presbytery, in the Romanesque style, while the central nave is in the Gothic style with a ribbed vault.
Section of the CM-4115 road through the Dehesa Labrada, good track and beautiful scenery.

It was the place where one of the oldest parcels in Campo de Calatrava, Caracuel, was founded in 1170. Even so, a few years later it was recovered by the Muslims until the victory of Alfonso VIII in Las Navas de Tolosa in 1212.

Mainly, the pentagonal tower of the castle stands out, which covers an older one, from the Almohad period. Today, remains of what was its square enclosure and even the remains of a cistern are preserved. We can access the castle through a narrow stone path that begins behind the Hermitage of Santo Cristo de Caracuel de Calatrava.

This town is located in the valley of the Jabalón river, a tributary of the Guadiana on its left bank. In addition, its municipal term is part of the center of the province.

It is characterized by having a landscape with a great presence, especially in the cool mountains of the southwest with gall oaks, pine forests and streams, and in the west, in the area known as the valley, with the presence of rebollos, strawberry trees, heather. Therefore, we will find richness and natural diversity throughout its term.
In the urban nucleus is the Palacio de Clavería from the 16th century, home to the clavero, the person who guarded the keys to the Convent of Calatrava.

Other religious constructions are the Church of San Jorge Mártir and the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Valle.
